failure_reason: D206 session gate on CEProcessRecoveredPending rejected: (1) P15 explicitly anticipates old-session pending fills with session-mismatch escape clause, (2) CEProcessRecoveredPending never sends to msgs_ce_to_ob so phantom CEFinalization claim is false, (3) diff conflicts with step 3 accepted proposal.

proposal.technique_name: multi-source vector clocking with per-source cursors
proposal.claim: CEProcessRecoveredPending lacks OB session freshness gate, allowing processing of pending fills from rolled-back OB sessions. Adding session guard eliminates stale-session MFR production.

key_findings: CEProcessRecoveredPending has msgs_ce_to_ob in UNCHANGED clause — no CEFinalization is ever produced for recovered pending fills
P15 escape clause 'pa.session /= ob_primary[pa.market]' is affirmative spec-design evidence that old-session fills are expected
No C1 property is violated by the described behavior
Diff conflicts with step 3 accepted proposal on CEProcessRecoveredPending (step 3 collapsed MFR+Snapshot to single MFR)

grounds.finding: No C1 property is violated by the described behavior. All relevant properties checked: P2, P4, P6, P9, P12, P15, P16, P17, P18, P19.
P15 contains explicit disjunct 'pa.session /= ob_primary[pa.market]' as an escape clause — old-session pending fills are expected to persist and be processed.
Phantom CEFinalization consequence does not hold. CEProcessRecoveredPending has msgs_ce_to_ob in UNCHANGED clause — recovered pending fills never trigger CEFinalization.
Diff conflicts with step 3 accepted proposal. Step 3 collapsed MFR+Snapshot to single MFR in CEProcessRecoveredPending. Both proposals cannot apply cleanly.
Fix introduces liveness hazard: CEProcessRecoveredPending blocked on ce_cached_ob_session until CEReceiveOBViewChange fires, creating a message ordering dependency absent from the current spec.

judgment.next_step_guidance: Do not re-propose a session freshness gate on CEProcessRecoveredPending without first identifying a concrete C1 property violated by old-session fill processing. The P15 escape clause must be addressed.
